---
title: "Sheet 10 — solutions"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

# Sheet 10 — solutions

```{r}
out <- read.csv("data/outcome-of-care-measures.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
hos <- read.csv("data/hospital-data.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
out$ha <- as.numeric(out[[11]])
out$hf <- as.numeric(out[[17]])
out$pn <- as.numeric(out[[23]])
library(dplyr)
```

**Ex. 1.** → **4706**
```{r}
j <- inner_join(out, hos, by = "Provider.Number")
nrow(j)
```

**Ex. 2.** → **120** hospitals in `hos` without results; **0** `out` records without a match.
```{r}
nrow(anti_join(hos, out, by = "Provider.Number")) # 120
nrow(anti_join(out, hos, by = "Provider.Number")) # 0
```

**Ex. 3.** → **Acute Care Hospitals** (≈ 12.0)
```{r}
j %>%
  filter(!is.na(pn)) %>%
  group_by(Hospital.Type) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), pn = mean(pn)) %>%
  arrange(pn)
# Acute Care 12.0 | VA 12.1 | Critical Access 12.4
```

**Ex. 4.** → **Government Federal** (≈ 10.9)
```{r}
j %>%
  filter(!is.na(hf)) %>%
  group_by(Hospital.Ownership) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), hf = mean(hf)) %>%
  arrange(hf) %>%
  head(1)
```

**Ex. 5.** → **53 out of 54 states**
```{r}
best <- out %>%
  filter(!is.na(ha)) %>%
  group_by(State) %>%
  slice_min(ha, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  inner_join(hos, by = "Provider.Number")
nrow(best) # 54 (states + territories)
sum(best$Hospital.Type == "Acute Care Hospitals") # 53
```

**Ex. 6.** → **rows of `a` that DO NOT have a match in `b`**.

**Ex. 7.** → **`group_by() %>% slice_min(x, n=1)`**.

**Ex. 8.** → **keeps all rows of `a`, appends `b`, missing values = NA**.
